news.headline: AFM investigation into Azerion trading irregularities concludes without penalties for company or major shareholders.
news.summary: On 14 March 2025 in Amsterdam, Azerion disclosed that the Autoriteit Financiële Markten (AFM) has finalized its probe into trading irregularities concerning Azerion shares as previously disclosed in a press release on 13 March 2023. Following the investigation, the AFM decided not to impose any penalties on Azerion or its primary stakeholders. The AFM issued a statement today regarding the matter.

news.summary: In the latest data released, it was reported that the unemployment rate has dropped to 3.8%, the lowest it has been in the last decade. This decrease represents approximately 250,000 more people finding employment. Additionally, the economy has seen significant growth, with a 4.5% increase in GDP last quarter. The stock market has also experienced a notable boost, with the main index rising by 15% in the last year. These positive indicators suggest a strong and thriving economy, benefiting both the job market and investors.

news.headline: Aegon launches community investment program with Bermuda Foundation, focusing on education, financial empowerment, and grant support. Strengthening ties with Bermuda.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, The Hague witnessed the unveiling of Aegon’s latest initiative – a community investment program aimed at bolstering the Bermuda community. This move aligns seamlessly with Aegon’s ethos of empowering individuals to lead fulfilling lives. The comprehensive program comprises three vital elements: educational initiatives, financial and social empowerment projects, along with a substantial grant earmarked for the Bermuda Foundation. This venture serves to fortify Aegon's longstanding ties with Bermuda, originating two decades back with the establishment of Transamerica Life Bermuda, an Aegon subsidiary. The bond was further reinforced by Aegon's relocation of its legal headquarters to Bermuda in September 2023.

news.headline: CoreWeave and Bulk Infrastructure team up for one of Europe's largest NVIDIA AI deployments at N01 Datacenter Campus.
news.summary: Norwegian company CoreWeave, known as an AI Hyperscaler™, and Bulk Infrastructure are partnering to create a major NVIDIA AI deployment in Europe. This collaboration will take place at the N01 Datacenter Campus in Norway. This venture is set to become one of the largest NVIDIA AI deployments in the region. The partnership between CoreWeave and Bulk Infrastructure signifies a significant step in fostering advanced AI capabilities, thereby enhancing digital infrastructure. The decision to establish this deployment in Europe reflects a strategic move to bolster AI technology in the region.

137th Canton Fair promoting trade ties in Middle East through successful events in Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE. Largest fair in China.
news.summary: In April 2025, the 137th edition of the Canton Fair, known as China's largest trade fair, is set to take place in Guangzhou. Preparations for the fair have been underway, including a successful Middle East roadshow in February, which featured trade promotion events in Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates. This annual fair serves as a crucial platform for businesses to showcase their products and services, with a wide range of industries represented. The previous edition of the fair attracted over 200,000 buyers and garnered significant attention from both domestic and international participants.

news.headline: NORBIT's segment PIR secures NOK 260 million orders from defence and security sector client. Deliveries to start immediately.
news.summary: In an announcement made in Trondheim on 14th March 2025, NORBIT reported that its PIR segment secured orders valued at around NOK 260 million from a confidential contract manufacturing partner operating in the defense and security field. The delivery process for these contracts will begin promptly, with a significant portion of the orders set for completion in the second quarter of 2025.

news.headline: US court approves Three Arrows to raise FTX claim to $1.53B from $120M, rejecting FTX's objections and citing delays in sharing info.
news.summary: A US bankruptcy court has approved an increase in the claim by liquidators of Three Arrows Capital (3AC) against FTX from $120 million to $1.53 billion. Chief Judge John Dorsey dismissed FTX's objections stating that the delay in amending the claim was due to FTX's failure to provide relevant information promptly. 3AC liquidators alleged breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and breach of fiduciary duty, claiming FTX held $1.53 billion in hedge fund assets liquidated to settle $1.33 billion in liabilities. Three Arrows Capital was one of the industry's largest crypto hedge funds with $3 billion in assets before its June 2022 collapse. FTX has also filed lawsuits to reclaim funds, including ones against SkyBridge Capital, Binance, and Waves founder. FTX filed for bankruptcy in November 2022.

news.headline: Basilea receives milestone payment due to robust Cresemba® (isavuconazole) sales in Asia Pacific and China.
Hong Kong Marriage Equality introduces "Proud Pair" to reshape traditions for diverse gender groups with jewelry.
news.summary: A historic 700-year-old tradition is undergoing transformation to embrace diversity in gender groups, with Hong Kong Marriage Equality teaming up with local jewellery brand Playback. The collaboration aims to promote equal marriage and spread love without boundaries. This initiative is set in motion in Hong Kong on March 14, 2025.

Trump cancels plan to double Canadian metal tariffs from 25% to 50% after Ontario removes charge on US electricity.
news.summary: The President had considered imposing a 50 percent tariff on Canadian metals; however, he decided against it following Ontario's removal of a charge on U.S. electricity.

news.headline: Trump's new tariffs on steel and aluminum, at 25%, cause global trade tensions as he aims to protect US economy.
news.summary: The recent move by President Trump saw the implementation of a 25 percent tariff on imports of metal, leading to escalating trade disputes worldwide. This action was taken as part of efforts to protect the U.S. economy from international competition.

news.headline: Galderma places EUR 500M Eurobond and CHF 435M bond, diversifies funding, reduces interest costs.
news.summary: Galderma Group AG, listed as SWX:GALD, operating in Zug, Switzerland, has reported notable advancements in its refinancing efforts. The company (SIX: GALD) has effectively issued a EUR 500 million bond with a fixed interest rate of 3.5% and a maturity period of 5 years. This move is part of their strategy to extend their maturity profile, broaden their funding channels, and lower interest charges, showcasing their commitment to financial stability and growth. The bond issuance is not intended for distribution in the United States or any jurisdiction where such actions are restricted by applicable laws or regulations.

news.headline: MEXC lists AO with a 140,000 USDT prize pool, expanding support for decentralized computing and AI innovation.
news.summary: Victoria, Seychelles - On March 14, 2025, MEXC, a prominent global cryptocurrency exchange, revealed its plans to list AO (AO) on the spot and futures markets starting at 06:00 (UTC). In celebration of this development, MEXC is initiating an Airdrop+ rewards event.

news.headline: Idorsia reports positive data on daridorexant's efficacy for chronic insomnia and nocturia in patients aged 55+.
news.summary: In Allschwil, Switzerland on March 14, 2025, Idorsia Ltd (SIX: IDIA) released a study titled “A randomized cross-over trial of daridorexant for the treatment of chronic insomnia and nocturia” in the Journal of Sleep Research. The report showcases the effectiveness of Idorsia’s daridorexant (QUVIVIQ™) at a 50 mg daily dose in individuals aged 55 years and above who have chronic insomnia and co-existing nocturia. The data highlights positive outcomes on symptoms for both conditions, enhancement in daytime functioning, and a favorable safety profile.

news.headline: Facephi excels in the DHS RIVTD Track 3 Evaluation in the USA, showcasing impressive performance.
news.summary: Facephi has achieved exceptional results in the RIVTD Track 3 Evaluation conducted by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) of the United States. The evaluation, which focused on testing the performance of facial recognition technology, showcased impressive outcomes for Facephi's solutions. This recognition highlights the company's expertise and capabilities in the field, solidifying its reputation as a leader in the industry. The DHS evaluation serves as a testament to Facephi's dedication to innovation and excellence, positioning the company as a key player in the fast-evolving landscape of facial recognition technology.

Texas court declares default judgment against Bancor DAO after it ignored summons, investors claim USD tens of millions losses due to liquidity issues. DAO failed to defend itself, faces lawsuit for deceiving about ILP mechanism and unregistered security token. Case reopened after previous dismissal due to protocol developers not US-based. Plaintiffs argue DAO is unincorporated partnership; total value locked stands at $38M. Other similar cases set precedence for suing DAOs for unregistered securities.
news.summary: A federal judge in Texas has issued a default judgment against Bancor DAO for failing to respond to a class action lawsuit filed by investors in May 2023. The lawsuit claims that investors lost tens of millions of dollars due to Bancor's failure to warn about liquidity issues during a 2022 withdrawal spike. Plaintiffs allege that Bancor deceived investors about its impermanent loss protection mechanism and misrepresented its token as an unregistered security. The lawsuit also argues that Bancor DAO, described as an unincorporated general partnership, could be sued in that capacity. The case was previously dismissed but later reopened in December. Bancor is a decentralized exchange protocol with $38 million in total value locked, down 98% from its peak in May 2021. The ruling sets a precedent for similar cases involving decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) and unregistered securities.

news.headline: Earnings for Inspired Entertainment to be released on March 17. Expected earnings per share: 15 cents. Quarterly revenue: $79.19 million.
news.summary: Inspired Entertainment, Inc. (NASDAQ: INSE) will unveil fourth-quarter financial results before trading begins on Monday, March 17. Analysts anticipate the New York-based company will announce earnings of 15 cents per share, a decrease from 17 cents per share from the previous year. Inspired Entertainment is expected to report quarterly revenue reaching $79.19 million, down from $81.2 million in the corresponding period last year as per data from Benzinga Pro. On March 10, Inspired Entertainment disclosed an expanded partnership with Rush Street Interactive, Inc. (NYSE: RSI). The company's shares experienced a 6.4% decline, closing at $8.03 on Thursday. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
